## 3dslibris 2.6.0

Navigation improvements and a fast metadata cache.

### New

- **ZL/ZR page flip (New 3DS only)**: ZL advances to the next page (same as R), ZR goes back (same as L). Orientation-aware: the mapping flips automatically when the display is rotated. See [#63](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/63).
- **D-pad wraps to next/previous page at list boundaries**: in list view, pressing Down on the last item jumps to the next page; pressing Up on the first item jumps to the previous page. In gallery view the same applies column-wise — pressing Right from the right column advances a page, Left from the left column goes back. See [#64](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/64).
- **Separated Circle Pad and D-pad controls**: reader, browser, and paged menus now use explicit mappings instead of the mixed libctru directional aliases, so Circle Pad and physical D-pad can behave differently where needed. For example, the reader now uses the Circle Pad for smooth panning in fixed-layout formats, while the D-pad retains the original page-turning behaviour. It also supports the New 3DS stick. See [#57](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/57).
- **Inline internal link navigation**: hold `Y` on a page with inline links to enter link focus mode, move with the D-pad, and press `A` to follow the selected link. See [#27](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/27).
- **Alternative SD data path support**: runtime data can now also be loaded from `sdmc:/config/3dslibris` in addition to the legacy `sdmc:/3ds/3dslibris` layout. See [#6](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/6).
- **Visual feedback**: added "saving cache..." status screens during the page cache write phase for EPUB and MOBI, paired with "loading..." and the progress indicator during the open phase.
- **Reset to defaults**: the `GENERAL` settings screen now has a **reset settings** option (on page 2, navigate with L/R or the **next** footer button) that restores font size, paragraph spacing, orientation, color mode, clock format, and library view to their factory defaults.
- **Clear all caches**: the `GENERAL` settings screen now has a **clear cache** option (page 2) that deletes all epub, mobi, cover, and metadata disk caches so everything is rebuilt fresh on the next open.

### Performance

- **Metadata disk cache (~60–80× faster per book)**: title, author, and cover path are now written to a small binary cache file (`cache/meta/*.bmc`) the first time a book is indexed. On subsequent launches the cache is read in ~1–2 ms per book instead of the previous 115–163 ms required to parse the source file. The cache key is derived from the file path, size, and mtime, so stale entries are ignored automatically when the source file changes.
- **Library opens fully populated (~300 ms for a 147-book library on warm cache)**: metadata for all books is now pre-loaded from the disk cache during the "Preparing library..." phase, before the browser opens. This also means the initial title sort uses real book titles instead of empty strings. On a cold cache, the failed lookups return in under 1 ms each and the behaviour is unchanged.
- **Faster first EPUB opens**: first-time EPUB pagination no longer writes the page cache during the spine parse, and common LTR text now uses a lighter shaping path while keeping the full BIDI/Arabic path for RTL content. 
- **MOBI page cache saves immediately at first open with visual feedback**: MOBI page cache saving no longer blocks at 100% during parsing. The write happens right after parsing completes.
- **Page cache saves are dramatically faster for large books**: the SD card write buffer for EPUB and MOBI page caches was increased from 32 KB to 256 KB, reducing the number of physical write operations per save. For a 9,919-page EPUB the cold-cache save time dropped from ~7 minutes to under 10 seconds (~45× faster).

### Fixes

- **Homebrew shutdown is now deterministic again**: `.3dsx` exits now finish app cleanup before terminating through the homebrew-safe path, avoiding the invalid APT shutdown sequence that could still trigger a crash or undefined exit behaviour after the fixes for HOME-time loading. See [#58](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/58).
- **Async opening progress stays main-thread safe**: the async open state is now installed before the worker is submitted, preventing worker-side progress callbacks from falling back to direct splash rendering.
- **Reader images no longer slip under the bottom HUD**: full-page covers and other large inline images now stop at the start of the status bar area, which also avoids some lower-edge content being clipped. See [#59](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/59).
- **Text alignment is now consistent across EPUB documents**: fixed three independent bugs that caused unreliable text centering and right-alignment — alignment declared via a CSS class was silently ignored; the first line of a paragraph continuing onto a new page lost its alignment and reverted to left; and line-width calculations for center/right positioning were thrown off by inline style tokens (bold/italic toggles, headings, `<small>`), causing text to appear at the wrong horizontal offset. See [#66](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/66).
- **EPUB headings now honor CSS font sizes and wrap correctly**: `h1`-`h6` now respect heading-specific `font-size` styling from inline CSS and stylesheet classes, including relative units, and large headings paginate without corrupting line breaks or leaking their size/bold styling into the following paragraph. The same EPUB CSS class map path now also applies `vertical-align: super/sub` rules declared in stylesheet classes, so superscript and subscript styling no longer depends on inline markup alone. See [#46](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/46).
- **EPUB lists now respect CSS marker suppression on `<li>` items**: list bullets/ordinals are now correctly hidden when `list-style-type: none` is declared through stylesheet classes on the list item itself, instead of only through inherited parent-list context. See [#48](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/48).

### Packaging

- **SD data compatibility**: installs that keep data under `sdmc:/config/3dslibris` are now supported alongside the existing `sdmc:/3ds/3dslibris` layout. See [#6](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/6).

---

## ❤️ Community Shoutouts

3dslibris wouldn't be the same without your support! This version is dedicated to:

* **Fueling the Code:** A special thank you to my **Ko-fi supporters**. Your donations help keep the project going and keep me caffeinated!
* **Enhancement Ideas:** Thanks to @Kr0Key, @Narwher,  @EmbersFlying and @The0zymandias for their suggestions and feedback that helped shape this release.

### Included assets

- `3dslibris.cia`
- `3dslibris-debug.cia`
- `3dslibris.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-debug.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-sdmc.zip` (runtime files only; pair it with the `.3dsx` asset for Homebrew Launcher installs)
- `3dslibris-source.tar.gz`
